question: What's the best time to visit Tin Can Bay QLD with kids?
answer: G'day! The best time to visit Tin Can Bay with the little ones is during the cooler months from April to October. You'll dodge the stinking hot summer humidity and enjoy perfect beach weather. The famous dolphin feeding happens year-round at 8am daily, so the kids won't miss out whenever you visit. School holidays get busy, so book early if you're planning a term break trip!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Tin Can Bay QLD?
answer: You betcha! Several holiday parks in Tin Can Bay welcome your furry family members. Tin Can Bay Tourist Park and some cabin-style accommodations are pet-friendly, though you'll need to book specific pet-allowed sites. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and any additional fees. Most require dogs to be leashed and well-behaved. Your pooch will love the nearby dog-friendly beaches too!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Tin Can Bay QLD during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay between $180-$350 per night for a family cabin, depending on size and amenities. Powered caravan sites are more budget-friendly at $50-$80 per night. Unpowered sites start around $35-$50. Easter and Christmas holidays are priciest. Book at least 3-6 months ahead for school holiday periods to snag the best rates and availability!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Pack beach essentials: swimmers, rashies, sunscreen (SPF50+), hats, and beach toys. Bring insect repellent for mozzies! Most cabins have basic cookware, but pack your own tea towels and condiments. Don't forget kids' bikes or scooters for getting around the park. Beach shoes protect little feet from shells and bluebottles. A portable high chair and night light are lifesavers for toddlers!

question: What is there to do near Tin Can Bay QLD holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps of family fun! The dolphin feeding experience at Barnacles is unmissable – wild dolphins visit daily. Kids love the calm, shallow beaches perfect for littlies. Carlo Sandblow's just 20 minutes away for sandboarding adventures. Try fishing off the jetty, hire a tinnie for exploring, or visit nearby Rainbow Beach. The Norman Point boat ramp area's great for spotting wildlife too!
